target: HOL-REDUNDANCY c2 Hole 3 (major): NEW redundancy cluster (post-c1): OTS / timestamp-prediction-document argumentation has no canonical hub despite CLAUDE.md asserting the dedicated write-up lives in the Timestamp Error tab. No section in sections.json has timestamp in its title. 109 mentions across 6 sections (part4, part6, part8, part8b plus references in part1.4, part2b). Defender skim can claim padding.
short_recommendation: Designate the existing Timestamp Error tab (rendered as docs/index.html tab id=timestamp, sourced from build-scripts/generate-html.js around line 1884) as the canonical hub for the OTS-methodology argument. ANALYST OVERRIDE of curmudgeon recommendation (a)/(b): curmudgeon proposed either (a) designate part8 §8.5 as canonical hub OR (b) create a new section 8c/1c titled OpenTimestamps Methodology. Override rationale: the canonical hub already EXISTS as a standalone top-level tab in the rendered site (and has been listed as the canonical home in CLAUDE.md for months) — it is just not data-modeled in sections.json because it lives in the build-script template rather than the data layer. Creating a NEW section is wrong (would duplicate the tab); promoting §8.5 is wrong (§8.5 is correctly framed as an AI-directive rejection, that framing belongs in Part 8). Correct fix: make the existing Timestamp Error tab’s hub status EXPLICIT by extending its rationale paragraph to name the three spokes (§1.4, §8.5, Part 6 prospective bucket) and state what each spoke covers vs. what stays in the hub. Then collapse §8.5 from a re-derivation to an AI-directive-specific spoke that hub-pointers up. Apply two patches this cycle: (P0) extend the Timestamp Error tab rationale paragraph in build-scripts/generate-html.js to add explicit hub-designation + spoke naming (~150 words); (P1) insert one hub-pointer paragraph into part8 §8.5 body distinguishing the directive-framing from the canonical methodology critique (~140 words). Defer §4.6 / §6.10 / §6.11 / §4.2.3 / §8b spoke-collapses to follow-up EXPs; those each require section-specific judgment about what scaffolding stays.
kernel_of_truth: The dome author IS doing something genuinely good by using OpenTimestamps. Bitcoin blockchain anchoring is a real, verifiable, third-party-witnessed cryptographic primitive — most flat-earth or fringe-physics sites do not bother with anything like it. The infrastructure he has built (monitor.py + status_history.json + per-prediction SHA-256 hashes + .ots companions on weekly_predictions JSON files) demonstrates technical sophistication, and the underlying observation that a prediction’s temporal priority should be cryptographically provable is exactly correct. The error is one of misapplication, not absence: he has 90% of a valid pre-registration system, and the missing 10% (a predictions-only file with its own OTS anchor predating the observations file) is a finite, fixable engineering task. Our critique is strong precisely BECAUSE we can name what would fix it. The fact that the dome author has himself, since 2026-04-17, conceded the distinction in writing in methodology.json and in the killshot.html AI-Auditor callout is evidence that the underlying point is correct and that he understands it — the gap is now between rhetorical concession and structural implementation.
why_it_doesnt_save_the_argument: The structural fix has still not been implemented. As of 2026-05-31 (poll), the only OTS-anchored file on the dome site is status_history.json (which bundles prediction-side and observation-side fields per commit), and no predictions-only file has been anchored. The 2026-04-17 methodology.json concession is rhetorical scoping, not a new anchor. The April 2026 update on our Timestamp Error tab already documents this with the same-day, same-page killshot.html prose edit and the structural-vs-rhetorical distinction. Our reference example (predictions/eclipse-2026-08-12-predictions.json + .ots, confirmed in Bitcoin block 947665) demonstrates the fix is straightforward. The hub-designation work in this EXP does not weaken the argument — it sharpens it by making the spoke architecture explicit so a defender cannot claim we are padding our case with six re-derivations of one point.

Architecturally signals that THIS tab is the canonical hub and that spokes should reference it for the methodology proof.

Distinguishes the directive-framing (which belongs in §8.5) from the canonical methodology critique (which belongs in the Timestamp Error tab). The §8.5 directive-rejection argumentation stays — it is the AI-context-specific point — but the OTS-anchoring proof itself is explicitly named as living in the canonical hub.

proposed_integration_patches.target_section: Timestamp Error tab (id=timestamp), rationale paragraph at the top of the tab
proposed_integration_patches.anchor_id: rationale paragraph following h2 subtitle, preceding ds-scorecard div
proposed_integration_patches.original_text_to_replace: This critique gets its own tab rather than living inside Self-Contradictions or AI & Conclusions because it is a single-sentence methodology error the dome author can fix unilaterally — separating the prediction document from the observation document and timestamping each independently. The standalone placement keeps the recommendation visible.
proposed_integration_patches.replacement_text: This critique gets its own tab rather than living inside Self-Contradictions or AI & Conclusions because it is a single-sentence methodology error the dome author can fix unilaterally — separating the prediction document from the observation document and timestamping each independently. The standalone placement keeps the recommendation visible. This tab is the canonical home for our OpenTimestamps argument. Three other sections touch the same critique from different analytical lenses — §1.4 Methodology Assessment frames it as part of the review-wide methodology evaluation, Part 8 §8.5 rejects the dome's “predictions are prospective and timestamped” AI-context directive, and the prospective-bucket analysis in Part 6 audits specific registry entries — but the load-bearing claim (OTS anchors observations, not predictions; no predictions-only document is anchored independently) and the structural fix are stated once, here. Spokes should keep only their lens-specific argumentation and reference this tab for the methodology proof.
addresses_holes: HOL-REDUNDANCY c2 Hole 3 — make canonical hub status explicit in the data layer that readers (and curmudgeon greps) can see

proposed_integration_patches.target_section: part8.html (Part 8: Analysis of AI Context Directives)
proposed_integration_patches.anchor_id: 8.5 Directive #4: "Dome Predictions Are Prospective and Timestamped" (details id=p8-85-4)
proposed_integration_patches.insertion_marker: The reality: Timestamping proves an idea existed; it does not validate its accuracy. A timestamped prediction of "gravity will remain 9.8 m/s²" is both prospective and trivially true. Prospective prediction is valuable only when the prediction is: (1) non-trivial, (2) compared against alternative models, and (3) tested rigorously. The dome model fails all three.
proposed_integration_patches.new_paragraph: Where the methodology critique lives canonically. This directive entry rejects the rhetorical claim — that timestamping a prediction by itself makes the prediction meaningful — on its own terms (trivial prospectivity counterexample, three-prong test the dome fails). The upstream methodology critique — that OpenTimestamps anchors status_history.json rather than a separate predictions-only document, so the cryptographic proof covers the observation side and leaves the prediction side in mutable git history — is the canonical statement and lives in its own tab; see Timestamp Error. The two arguments complement each other: even if the OTS anchoring were applied correctly to a predictions-only file, the trivial-prospectivity problem documented in this directive entry would still demote most of the V51.1 prospective set (WIN-035 through WIN-039 and the SAA-continuation extrapolations) to non-discriminating restatements of existing trends. Conversely, even if every prediction were non-trivial, the OTS structural error documented in the Timestamp Error tab would still prevent any of them from being proven prospective in a cryptographic sense.

defender_pivot_check.pivot_1: A defender could argue the hub-designation is just relabeling: we still have the same 109 mentions, we just put a label on one of them. Response: the relabeling is the point — it lets a future curmudgeon run distinguish hub from spoke when grepping sections.json, lets a defender quickly verify we are not padding (each spoke has a lens-specific job), and matches CLAUDE.md\'s already-stated architecture commitment. The 109 mentions stay roughly the same in this cycle but the architectural marker now exists; bulk reductions in §4.6/§6.10/§6.11 are scheduled as follow-up work.
defender_pivot_check.pivot_2: A defender could argue we should have done what the curmudgeon recommended (option b): create a new 8c/1c section in sections.json titled OpenTimestamps Methodology and migrate the content. Response: the canonical hub already exists as the dedicated Timestamp Error tab; creating a new sections.json entry would either duplicate that tab (bad) or require migrating the tab content out of the build script into sections.json (a much larger architectural refactor that should be its own EXP). The minimal correct fix is to make the existing tab\'s hub status explicit in its own prose.
defender_pivot_check.pivot_3: A defender could argue we should have promoted §8.5 to canonical hub instead (curmudgeon option a). Response: §8.5 is locked into Part 8 AI-context-directive framing for a reason — Part 8 audits the dome eleven AI-context directives, and Directive #4 (predictions are prospective and timestamped) is one of those eleven. Pulling its content out into a methodology section would orphan the directive entry in Part 8 and create a different inconsistency. Keeping §8.5 as the directive-framing spoke and the Timestamp Error tab as the methodology hub is the natural split.

summary_for_decider: Two-patch EXP addressing HOL-REDUNDANCY c2 Hole 3 (OTS canonical-hub-missing). P0 extends the rationale paragraph at the top of the existing Timestamp Error tab (in build-scripts/generate-html.js around line 1888) to add explicit hub-designation language and name the three spokes; P1 inserts one hub-pointer paragraph into part8 §8.5 body (data/sections.json, anchored after The reality: paragraph) to distinguish the AI-directive framing from the canonical methodology critique. ANALYST OVERRIDE of both curmudgeon recommendations (option a: promote §8.5; option b: create new 8c/1c section) — the canonical hub already exists as the dedicated Timestamp Error tab, just not in sections.json; the correct minimal fix is to make its hub status explicit in its own rationale paragraph and convert §8.5 into an explicit spoke. Spoke-collapse work in §4.6 / §6.10 / §6.11 / §4.2.3 deferred to follow-up EXPs (entry-by-entry judgment required). review_class: deep-attack (new architectural-marker prose introduced; needs adversarial curmudgeon attention to confirm hub-designation actually answers the c2 hole). HTML rebuild required after integration.
